prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for furan (C₄H₄O, 9 atoms) using DFT at the tier 'standard' level — B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0 singlet for the neutral state and charge ±1 doublets for the cation and anion. The ground-state geometry was used as-is from the input file. SCF converged for all three charge states. The Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor were computed from the atomic charge differences between neutral, cation, and anion states using Mulliken population analysis. The results show that the β-carbons (C4 and C5, atoms 4–5) are the most electrophilic and nucleophilic sites, with f⁺ = 1.924 and f⁻ ≈ 0.162, respectively. The dual descriptor identifies these carbons as preferred sites for electrophilic attack, consistent with furan's reactivity as a diene. All charge conservation checks passed (sum of f⁺ and f⁻ both = 1.0000). A visualization was generated. The integrity check returned status 'ok' and trustworthy = true.</prose>
<parameter name="n_atoms">9
